special_mechanics.name: Boomerang Attack
special_mechanics.description: NOVA is armed with a boomerang that can be fired in all eight directions, a rarity for NES/Famicom platformers. Can be upgraded for power, range, and quantity.

special_mechanics.name: Power Suit
special_mechanics.description: Equippable helmet item that allows NOVA to shoot energy blasts called 'Power Blades' in any of 8 directions through most surfaces.

special_mechanics.name: Fall Damage
special_mechanics.description: Unique to Power Blade: if the player falls off the screen they die immediately. Power Blazer and Power Blade 2 simply return player to previous area.

special_mechanics.name: Power-up System
special_mechanics.description: Collectible upgrades throughout sectors that improve boomerang power, range, and number of simultaneous projectiles.

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. No specific physics values documented in Data Crystal, TCRF, or Wikipedia sources. Available data focuses on gameplay mechanics (boomerang combat, power-ups, stage structure) and localization differences between Power Blazer (JP) and Power Blade (US/EU), not movement physics parameters.
sources_used: data_crystal - Power Blade article (mapper/ROM info only, no physics)
tcrf - Power Blade article (gameplay mechanics, localization, no physics parameters)
wikipedia - Power Blade article (gameplay overview, no movement physics)
